item.title: 'Reviving' an old Macbook with Arch Linux
item.description: This entire debacle began when I recieved an almost decade old Macbook Pro. To be precise, one from Mid 2014 with a 4th gen i5 processor, 8 GB DDR3 RAM, and a 128 GB SSD. Apart from a degenerating battery and faulty speakers, it was quite functional. The only problem? Factory restarting it and losing my Big Sur install. Regardless of what I then tried, it refused to update. The archaic UI and lack of software support was jarring, but bearable.

item.title: About
item.description: I’m currently a CS student. This is my personal website. I will be writing about Linux here primarily, but also other paraphernalia. I enjoy tinkering with my devices till they’re just the way I want them, listening to abhorrent music and reading obnoxious literature.
